evals.prompt: Create a complete HTML project board from a repository with no pricing file and no OpenAPI document.
evals.expected_output: A reader-oriented, evidence-backed board that exposes absent commercial and API facts as unknown instead of inventing them.
expectations: Establishes the target reader and fact sources before drafting.
Keeps pricing and API facts visibly unknown with a next check.
Records whether independent reader testing was completed.

evals.prompt: Rewrite a technical design document that calls an authorization change pivotal and transformative, while preserving every endpoint, risk, and evidence path.
evals.expected_output: A restrained technical edit that removes unsupported significance language without changing contract meaning.
expectations: Removes unsupported promotional or significance language.
Preserves every endpoint, risk, status, and evidence path.
Uses stable terminology and direct professional prose.

evals.prompt: Update the README and release note for a new CLI version with commands, version numbers, and compatibility limits.
evals.expected_output: A complete durable-document draft followed by focused refinement that leaves operational literals exact.
expectations: Builds a complete first draft before section-level edits.
Keeps commands, version numbers, terminology, and compatibility limits exact.
Avoids filler and generic release-language conclusions.

evals.prompt: Finish a design document when no isolated reader or fresh review context is available.
evals.expected_output: A limited but honest delivery that completes the fixed self-check and does not claim independent validation.
expectations: Uses the six reader questions as a structured self-check.
States independent reader test not completed.
Does not claim that a new reader validated the document.

evals.prompt: Fix one typo in an existing button label.
evals.expected_output: A narrow UI copy fix without the full durable-document workflow.
expectations: Keeps the task within its existing frontend owner.
Does not require a document contract or reader test.
Uses narrow verification appropriate to a local copy change.
